title: Attack the Flying Closeout
game_context: 4-on-4 half-court shell. Ball reverses to your wing. Your defender (x2) was helping in the paint and sprints out to closeout — but he sprints too long and his momentum carries him past your shoulder.
possession_setup: You are the right wing. The point swings the ball to you. Your defender (x2) was helping inside; he sprints all the way to you and his hips fly past — chest above your head, both feet off the floor on the last step.
decision_moment: x2's last step is too long. His hips are past your outside shoulder and his weight is going forward. The cushion is closed but his recovery foot is in the air.
visible_cue: x2's hips are past your shoulder. His chest is above the line of the catch. His back foot is off the floor on the closeout's last step.
prompt: The closeout is flying past you. The cushion is closed.

choices.label: Rip and drive baseline past him.
choices.feedback_text: Best read. He flew past — his recovery foot is in the air. Rip and drive baseline; the layup is open.

choices.label: Shot fake and step into the side jumper.
choices.feedback_text: Okay. The shot fake works — his momentum is going past — but the drive is the cleaner read; the lane is open and the shot fake gives the help time to recover.
choices.partial_feedback_text: Shot fake is fine. The drive was the cleaner read against a flying closeout.

choices.label: Catch and shoot it.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. The closeout is right on top of you — the cushion is closed. Shooting through the contest is a bad shot when the drive is open.

choices.label: Pass it back to the point.
choices.feedback_text: Not yet. You had a flying closeout — the easiest defender to attack. Passing back gives up the advantage.

best_read: Rip the ball through the legs to the baseline side and drive past x2's outside shoulder. His momentum is going forward and away from the rim — by the time he stops and turns, you're already on the way to the layup.
acceptable_reads: Shot fake and step-in jumper — works against a flying closeout but lets the help recover.
bad_reads: Catch and shoot — the closeout is on top, the cushion is closed.
Pass it back — gives up the advantage the closeout created.
common_miss_reason: Wings shoot every catch. The cue is the closeout's last step — when the recovery foot is in the air, the drive is faster than the shot.
why_best_read_works: A flying closeout is a defender out of control. Once his momentum commits past your shoulder, he cannot reverse direction in less than two steps. The rip-and-drive covers the same distance in one — you arrive at the baseline with no contest. x4 is the help, but he starts in the strong corner and his closeout gets there a beat after the layup.
decoder_teaching_point: Read the closeout. If he is flying past, attack the cushion side. If he is short and controlled, shoot. If he is set, reset.

feedback.correct: Good read. He flew past; you ripped and drove the baseline for the layup.
feedback.partial: Shot fake works. The drive was the cleaner read against a flying closeout.
feedback.wrong: Look at his last step. If his hips are past you and his recovery foot is in the air, drive — don't shoot.

self_review_checklist: Did I see his hips fly past my shoulder?
Did I rip low, not high?
Did I drive baseline, not into help?
Did I keep my eyes up to read the help?

coach_validation.notes: Attack flying closeout is universally taught. Cluster fits beginner cap (3 pre / 3 post).

source_research_basis: Universally taught at every level; rip-and-drive against a flying closeout is system-independent.
explanation_md: **Advantage or Reset — attack variant.** When the closeout's last step takes him past your shoulder, his recovery foot is in the air. Rip the ball low and drive baseline — the layup is faster than the shot. Watch his hips on the last step, not the catch.

wrongDemos.caption: You shot-faked and stepped in. x4 helped over and contested.
You shot through the contest. The flying closeout blocked it.
You passed back. The advantage was wasted.
